blocks.text: was already watching the house next door when she reached for her bag. She didn't say anything about it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Come look at the view from the back.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Julian. Who are you looking at?

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Your face does a specific thing when you say nobody and mean somebody.
The one with the loudest laugh hadn't unloaded a single bag. He'd already introduced himself to two sets of neighbors. We were next.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: One of them is going to come over.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: He's introduced himself to both properties on his left. We're next.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: And when he does, we say hello. Just hello.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: And you won't do the thing.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: The thing where you shake someone's hand and in three seconds you know how they take their coffee and what they're worried about.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I find it impressive. They'll find it unsettling.

blocks.prompt: He reads people for a living. Does he read her?

options.label: He doesn't look. Some things he leaves alone on purpose.
He looks. There's something in it he hasn't named yet.
